plannedScenes.title: setup scene 1
plannedScenes.dramaticPurpose: Open the episode through its immediate question: Can Kylie start over, feel wanted, and write under her own name in a city that is already watching her? Kylie arrives in Bucharest with two suitcases and her grandmother's address. She explores the streets of Bucharest and wanders into a bookshop owned by Stela who befriends her and introduces Kylie to the secret nightlife world of Valescu Club and her other friend Mika. After testing Kylie, the three become friends and form the Dusk Club. At a rooftop bar she catches the attention of a man in a charcoal suit and a rougher man near the kitchen. Walking home through Cismigiu, she is attacked and rescued by the impossibly handsome stranger, who walks her to her threshold and vanishes. At 4am she turns the night into the first Dating After Dusk post under the codename Mr. Midnight, and by evening the post has gone viral. The blog, Dusk Club, Victor's staged courtship, Stela's protection, Mika's placement, Radu's first sighting, and Kylie's first authored act all become live season anchors..

seasonPromiseContracts.sourceText: Sex and the City meets Twilight - dating can be a horror show in a land where the men are actual monsters.

A New York food writer fleeing a publicly cancelled engagement arrives in Bucharest, starts a dating blog under codenames like Mr. Midnight and The Mountain, and slowly discovers that the man she is writing about, the friends she is writing it for, and the city itself are all monsters of one kind or another.

Be the new It girl in a beautiful foreign city with a friend group, a viral dating blog, a gorgeous dangerous suitor, and a quieter slow-burn alternative, while realizing that being adored is not the same as being known.

worldTreatmentContracts.sourceText: Modern Bucharest looks like Belle Epoque facades, Communist blocks, third-wave coffee, grandmother soups, and a club scene with teeth; underneath, strigoi run nightlife and real estate, pricolici keep to the Carpathians, Romani practitioners keep women alive, and contracted succubi feed through the dating scene.
Present day, with phones, blogs, dating apps, content creators, brand deals, and modern Bucharest social life.
Strigoi need invitation to enter dwellings unless a threshold is broken, cannot be photographed, and cannot directly lie about their nature when asked
succubi are contracted and can only be freed by consented ritual before sunrise

folk practitioners can ward, warn, bind, scry, break contracts, and make rooms hostile to strigoi, but consent matters. A public codenamed blog about an unphotographable strigoi is uniquely dangerous because it gives Kylie a voice Victor cannot fully control.

A 34-year-old American food writer turned blogger, quarter-Romanian on her father's side, newly arrived in Bucharest after her engagement to New York restaurateur Daniel Hayes imploded publicly. Charming, wounded, quick-witted, and habitually observational — she orders second, watches the room, writes the piece later, and second-guesses herself until the important window has nearly closed.
Author her own life on her own terms, protect the authentic voice that finally belongs to her, and learn that being desired by someone powerful is not the same as being free.
Being chosen by a beautiful, powerful man is the same as being known and safe; her job is to observe and document other people's lives rather than claim her own appetites and author her own story.

Her publicly cancelled engagement to Daniel Hayes made her feel simultaneously humiliated in front of the world and privately unknown — desired for a surface that was never the real her. Her grandmother Veronica's unexplained escape from Bucharest in 1962 left the Marinescu line with an unfinished story Kylie has never been given permission to complete.

plannedScenes.title: development scene 2
plannedScenes.dramaticPurpose: Escalate the episode pressure through a concrete turn: Can Kylie start over, feel wanted, and write under her own name in a city that is already watching her? Kylie arrives in Bucharest with two suitcases and her grandmother's address. She explores the streets of Bucharest and wanders into a bookshop owned by Stela who befriends her and introduces Kylie to the secret nightlife world of Valescu Club and her other friend Mika. After testing Kylie, the three become friends and form the Dusk Club. At a rooftop bar she catches the attention of a man in a charcoal suit and a rougher man near the kitchen. Walking home through Cismigiu, she is attacked and rescued by the impossibly handsome stranger, who walks her to her threshold and vanishes. At 4am she turns the night into the first Dating After Dusk post under the codename Mr. Midnight, and by evening the post has gone viral. The blog, Dusk Club, Victor's staged courtship, Stela's protection, Mika's placement, Radu's first sighting, and Kylie's first authored act all become live season anchors..

plannedScenes.dramaticPurpose: Escalate the episode pressure through a concrete turn: Can Kylie start over, feel wanted, and write under her own name in a city that is already watching her? Kylie arrives in Bucharest with two suitcases and her grandmother's address. She explores the streets of Bucharest and wanders into a bookshop owned by Stela who befriends her and introduces Kylie to the secret nightlife world of Valescu Club and her other friend Mika. Walking home through Cismigiu, she is attacked and rescued by the impossibly handsome stranger, who walks her to her threshold and vanishes. At 4am she turns the night into the first Dating After Dusk post under the codename Mr. Midnight, and by evening the post has gone viral. The blog, Dusk Club, Victor's staged courtship, Stela's protection, Mika's placement, Radu's first sighting, and Kylie's first authored act all become live season anchors..

plannedScenes.title: development scene 6
plannedScenes.dramaticPurpose: Escalate the episode pressure through a concrete turn: Can Kylie start over, feel wanted, and write under her own name in a city that is already watching her? Kylie arrives in Bucharest with two suitcases and her grandmother's address. She explores the streets of Bucharest and wanders into a bookshop owned by Stela who befriends her and introduces Kylie to the secret nightlife world of Valescu Club and her other friend Mika. After testing Kylie, the three become friends and form the Dusk Club. At a rooftop bar she catches the attention of a man in a charcoal suit and a rougher man near the kitchen. Walking home through Cismigiu, she is attacked and rescued by the impossibly handsome stranger, who walks her to her threshold and vanishes. At 4am she turns the night into the first Dating After Dusk post under the codename Mr. Midnight, and by evening the post has gone viral. The blog, Dusk Club, Victor's staged courtship, Stela's protection, Mika's placement, Radu's first sighting, and Kylie's first authored act all become live season anchors..

mechanicPressure.storyPressure: Kylie arrives in Bucharest with two suitcases and her grandmother's address. She explores the streets of Bucharest and wanders into a bookshop owned by Stela who befriends her and introduces Kylie to the secret nightlife world of Valescu Club and her other friend Mika. After testing Kylie, the three become friends and form the Dusk Club. At a rooftop bar she catches the attention of a man in a charcoal suit and a rougher man near the kitchen. Walking home through Cismigiu, she is attacked and rescued by the impossibly handsome stranger, who walks her to her threshold and vanishes. At 4am she turns the night into the first Dating After Dusk post under the codename Mr. Midnight, and by evening the post has gone viral.

seasonPromiseContracts.sourceText: The first three episodes feel like vacation and dating-show glamour, with a single horror thread under the champagne. The middle episodes darken into supernatural revelation, blog warfare, and romantic disillusionment. The final episodes reveal one friend after another as something other than human, forcing Kylie to decide whether she keeps her voice, her friends, her name, and her self.
